learning_outcomes.description: Use their knowledge and expertise to evaluate biomanufacturing technologies and trouble shoot expression technologies.

Critically evaluate the environmental, economic, regulatory, and stakeholder considerations involved in new biomanufacturing processes, and synthesise these perspectives to inform evidence-based decision-making.

Critically assess the downstream processing requirements for large-scale biological manufacture, justifying the purpose, sequence, and trade-offs of each stage in the context of process efficiency and product quality.
Perform and analyse data generated using key analytical techniques critical in the evaluation of biomanufacturing products and procedures.
Interpret and apply current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP) requirements in biomanufacturing, and critically evaluate complex scenarios and case studies for regulatory compliance and risk.

assessments.task_description:
An in-class invigilated written exam will examine Modules 1 and 2 material.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.due_date:
23/10/2026 11:59 am

It is recommended that each group finalise and submit their practical report as soon as possible after completing the experimental work. A long delay between the laboratory classes and report preparation may result in the loss of important observations, contextual details, and insights that are not fully captured in laboratory notes.

Although the official submission deadline is later in the semester, groups are strongly encouraged to complete and submit their report within two weeks of finishing their experimental work. This will help ensure the report accurately reflects the experiments performed and the discussions held during the practical sessions.
assessments.task_description:
Beer Fermentation Practical Report.

The practical runs across the semester in weekly group rotations (~15 students per group), with Group 1 commencing in Week 2 (completing in Week 3) and subsequent groups following weekly until the final group begins in Week 7 and completes in Week 8. Reports are due at the end of Week 12. Students are strongly encouraged to prepare their reports promptly after completing the practical to ensure accuracy and retention of detail; delaying writing until Week 12 is not recommended. The mid-semester break may be used productively for report completion.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit your Structural Biology Prac Report through the course Turnitin submission point in Blackboard. Before submitting your assessment item to Turnitin please name your file in the following way. FAMILY NAME_GivenName_StudentID_CourseCode_AssignmentName For example: Bob Smith (ID: 54329876) would name his ABCD1234 essay 1 in the following way. SMITH_Bob_54329876_ABCD1234_Essay1
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 12(03 Aug - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Pilot-Scale Beer Fermentation Practical

Pilot-Scale Beer Fermentation Practical: From Week 2, students participate in a hands-on pilot-scale brewing practical in groups of approximately 15. Each group completes a 3-hour brewing session, including a brewhouse instrumentation tour and the brewing process (mashing, boiling, and inoculation), followed by a 1-hour analytical session the following week to monitor fermentation progress using key measurements such as optical density and specific gravity.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Pilot-Scale Beer Fermentation Practical Pilot-Scale Beer Fermentation Practical: From Week 2, students participate in a hands-on pilot-scale brewing practical in groups of approximately 15.
